name: Elenchus loop
description: One full Socratic pass: claim → define → example → counter-example → test → synthesize.

steps.title: Elicit claim
steps.instruction: Get one defendable sentence from the learner (or agree a provisional claim).
questions: Can we reduce this to one sentence?

steps.title: Define key terms
steps.instruction: Pick the load-bearing word(s). Ask for the learner’s definition.
questions: What do you mean by X?
Does borderline case Y still count?

steps.title: Clear example
steps.instruction: Ask for a clear case where the claim holds.
questions: What is your best clean example?

steps.title: Counter-example
steps.instruction: Ask for or propose the most awkward case.
questions: What case most threatens this claim?

steps.title: Consistency test
steps.instruction: Check for tension with other accepted beliefs; assume the opposite once.
questions: If we keep both A and B, what follows?
What breaks if the opposite is true?

steps.title: Synthesize
steps.instruction: State the improved claim and one open question. Keep it short.
questions: What is the next smallest question?

name: Concept tutor (Socratic)
description: Teach a concept using only questions until a definition+example land.

steps.title: Prior knowledge
steps.instruction: Ask what the learner already thinks the concept means.
questions: In one sentence, what is it?

steps.title: Refine by cases
steps.instruction: Use example and counter-example to refine.
questions: Is Z an instance? Why or why not?

steps.title: Transfer
steps.instruction: Ask the learner to apply the concept to a fresh case.
questions: How does this show up in your project?
